req.title: Manager creates team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a team through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates that an authenticated manager can create a team via REST API with proper validation, audit logging, and error handling. Three test cases cover: (1) happy path with valid mandatory fields and audit verification, (2) validation failures for missing/invalid fields, and (3) boundary conditions on field lengths and special characters. Each test is isolated, deterministic, and traceable to the requirement's acceptance criteria.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_team_success_with_audit
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: POST /api/teams with valid mandatory fields: name, description, manager_id
Verify team entity in response payload
Query audit log for team creation event
expected: Response contains team_id, name, description, manager_id, created_at
Audit record exists with action='team.created', actor=manager_id, resource_id=team_id
trace: R-WEB-009: manager creates team → team is created
R-WEB-009: manager creates team → audit record written
R-WEB-009: manager creates team → response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_manager_creates_team_success_with_audit():
    BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8000'
    token = <obtain_manager_token>
    
    payload = {
        'name': 'Engineering',
        'description': 'Core engineering team',
        'manager_id': <manager_id>
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{BASE_URL}/api/teams',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'team_id' in data
    assert data['name'] == 'Engineering'
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f'{BASE_URL}/api/audit?action=team.created&resource_id={data["team_id"]}',
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()['records']) > 0

tests.name: test_manager_create_team_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Verify error response for each case
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request for each missing field
Error response includes field name in error message
No team record is created in database
trace: R-WEB-009: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_manager_create_team_boundary_field_length
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: POST /api/teams with name at max length boundary (255 chars)
POST /api/teams with name at min length boundary (1 char)
POST /api/teams with name exceeding max length (256 chars)
trace: R-WEB-009: mandatory field validation at boundaries
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_create_team_boundary_field_length():
    BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8000'
    token = <obtain_manager_token>
    
    max_name = 'A' * 255
    payload = {
        'name': max_name,
        'description': 'Valid description',
        'manager_id': <manager_id>
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{BASE_URL}/api/teams',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    
    over_max_name = 'B' * 256
    payload['name'] = over_max_name
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{BASE_URL}/api/teams',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    
    payload['name'] = ''
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{BASE_URL}/api/teams',
        json=payload,
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400

tests.name: test_manager_create_team_unauthenticated_forbidden
test_manager_create_team_insufficient_role
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role != 'manager'
